room_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: zero enclosed volumes — furniture clusters sit on detached floor mats with no bounding partitions between kitchen/living, bedroom and second-bedroom groupings. No room is bounded because no walls exist.

global_visual.evidence: Judge 1: individual furniture models are clean with no broken/floating sub-geometry, but the scene lacks its architectural structure (walls/doors/windows/floor slab), leaving furniture suspended in void. Combined with Judge 2's missing grounding shadows, the whole-scene coherence is compromised even though per-object quality is acceptable.

wall_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1 (ESTRUTURA, high confidence): no wall geometry rendered anywhere; the entire architectural shell is absent, furniture floats on a blank backdrop. This alone forces the panel to FAIL.

scale_rotation.evidence: Judge 1: furniture shares a consistent isometric orientation and internally plausible relative scale (bed vs wardrobe vs dining set vs kitchen run all proportionate); no stretched, skewed, or mis-rotated objects.

window_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no wall-hosted glazing present; thin panels on the nightstands read as furniture decor, not architectural windows. Cannot be confirmed absent walls; no orphan glass or full-height void detected.

door_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no doors visible; with no host walls, placement cannot be validated and no floating/orphan doors are seen. Indeterminate due to the missing shell — not evidence of correctness.

material_light.evidence: Judge 2 (MATERIAL & LUZ, medium confidence): flat solid fills with per-face directional shading only — no texture maps (no wood grain, fabric weave, or specular) and no AO/contact/cast shadows, so furniture reads as floating. Exposure well-balanced and warm palette coherent, keeping it above a flat-shading fail but below render fidelity.

findings.location: entire model perimeter — all sides
findings.evidence: No wall geometry rendered at all; the entire enclosing shell is missing, leaving a fully non-enclosed perimeter with furniture floating in empty space (Judge 1). | FP-032: oracle FAIL degraded to WARN (backend not proven discriminative).

findings.location: all rooms — none bounded
findings.evidence: No enclosed room volumes exist because no walls are present; furniture sits on detached floor mats with no bounding partitions between clusters (Judge 1). | FP-032: oracle FAIL degraded to WARN (backend not proven discriminative).

findings.location: all furniture (kitchen cabinets top-left, wardrobe center-right, beds, sofa, dining chairs)
findings.evidence: Surfaces are uniform solid fills with only face-orientation shading; zero texture/material detail, characteristic of a flat-shaded placeholder rather than a rendered material (Judge 2).

findings.location: ground plane / tan floor mats under bed (center) and bed (right)
findings.evidence: No ambient-occlusion, contact, or cast shadows anywhere; objects sit on mats and grey background with no grounding shadow, so depth relies solely on flat per-face shading (Judge 2).

design_patterns_observed.pattern: paleta cálida coerente (warm_compact)
design_patterns_observed.why: Consistent warm browns/tans/beige with distinct, plausible per-object hues (dark-brown wardrobe, light-tan cabinets, black dining chairs) matching the 'warm' variant intent; no wrong-hue or blown-out exposure (Judge 2).

design_patterns_observed.pattern: escala e orientação isométrica coerente do mobiliário
design_patterns_observed.why: Furniture pieces share one isometric orientation with plausible relative sizing across bed/wardrobe/dining/kitchen; proportion and rotation are a solved axis and can be treated as a stable baseline (Judge 1 scale_rotation PASS).

design_patterns_observed.pattern: flat-shaded SU-free preview (sem texturas/sombras)
design_patterns_observed.why: Solid colors + per-face shading only, no GI/textures/cast shadows — expected for a SketchUp-free lightweight iso and likely a pipeline baseline, but it does not reach render-material fidelity (Judge 2).

design_patterns_observed.pattern: mobiliário sem shell arquitetônico (clusters flutuando no vazio)
design_patterns_observed.why: Cross-report inference: Judge 1 finds zero walls/rooms/floor slab while Judge 2 confirms only furniture is rendered and lacks contact shadows. Correct per-object geometry + palette cannot compensate for a dropped architectural shell — furniture reads as floating islands. Signals the enclosure-generation stage is missing or upstream of the furniture pass, not a material regression.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: ausência de sombra de contato → perda de ancoragem/profundidade
design_patterns_observed.why: With no AO/contact shadow the eye has no ground cue, compounding the missing-shell problem; even a lightweight contact-shadow pass would materially improve perceived grounding in flat-shaded previews (Judge 2).

scale_rotation.evidence: Judge 1: relative proportions across kitchen run, wardrobe, beds, dining set, armchair are consistent and read at coherent human scale; uniform furniture orientation in the iso, no gross over/undersizing or mis-rotation.

window_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no windows and no wall planes to host them; no full-height void or orphan glass wall panel present. Cannot assess. (Distinct from judge 2's furniture-attached mirror panels, tracked under material_light.)

global_visual.evidence: Both judges converge: this is a furniture-only SU-free iso lacking BOTH the structural shell (judge 1) and any rendered material/light pass (judge 2). The missing wall/door/window envelope makes the layout non-enclosed and structurally unverifiable against the PDF, and the flat-shaded surfaces cannot validate material fidelity.

wall_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1 (ESTRUTURA): no wall shell of any kind rendered — furniture and two isolated floor slabs float on a gray background with zero perimeter, partition, or wall segment. Corroborated by judge 2 noting a flat gray backdrop with no environment. Fully non-enclosed model.

room_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: two flat floor plates read as intended bedroom/suite footprints, but rooms are not enclosed — identity/separation is inferable only from furniture clustering, not from bounding walls.

door_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no doors and no host walls, so placement/swing cannot be assessed; no floating orphan door present either. Unverifiable rather than confirmed-bad.

material_light.evidence: Judge 2 (MATERIAL & LUZ, high conf): every surface is a solid flat diffuse fill with only SketchUp per-face normal darkening — no texture maps, no specular/gloss, no cast or contact shadows, flat ~#d0d0d3 backdrop. Palette and exposure are coherent, so the failure is texture/reflectance/shadow, not color. Mirror panels are inert pale blue-gray rectangles with no reflection. A placeholder look, not a V-Ray material pass.

findings.location: entire model perimeter — all four sides / every room boundary

findings.location: ground plane / all furniture bases against the gray background
findings.evidence: No cast shadows and no contact/ambient-occlusion shadows; every object sits on a featureless flat gray field and appears to float. No lighting/shadow pass applied. | FP-032: oracle FAIL degraded to WARN (backend not proven discriminative).

findings.location: vanity/dresser mirror panels — one right of the dining set, one at far-right bed
findings.evidence: Mirror/glass panels are flat pale blue-gray rectangles with no reflection, transparency, or frame highlight; furniture-attached so not fully orphaned, but material response is absent.

findings.location: whole scene
findings.evidence: Furniture-only SU-free iso with no structural shell AND no rendered material/light pass. Doors, windows, and room enclosure cannot be validated against the PDF because hosting geometry is absent, and material/light cannot be validated because only flat placeholder shading is present.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: warm-neutral compact palette (beige/tan/warm-brown casework + black accent chairs)
design_patterns_observed.why: Hues are internally consistent and warm-neutral with no clashing or wrong-tinted surfaces and exposure well within range. Palette/color selection is sound in isolation — the failure lies in missing texture/reflectance, not in the color choice.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: flat-shaded SU-free preview used as a stand-in for a rendered material pass
design_patterns_observed.why: Solid diffuse fills + per-face normal shading + no shadows + flat gray backdrop is an unmistakable placeholder; it cannot validate material/light fidelity against a V-Ray target.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: furniture-only iso with no wall shell, relying on furniture clustering to imply rooms
design_patterns_observed.why: Combining judge 1's absent perimeter with the two loose floor slabs: room identity and separation become inferable only from furniture grouping, which cannot substitute for wall enclosure and leaves all host-based openings (doors/windows) unverifiable.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: uniform iso orientation with coherent human-scale furniture proportions
design_patterns_observed.why: Even absent walls, cross-object proportions (kitchen run, wardrobe, beds, dining set, armchair) stay plausible at human scale with uniform orientation — a reusable positive for readability of a furniture layout independent of the render/material pass.

door_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: indeterminate. No door leaves or openings visible, but with no host walls a door cannot be validated; no floating orphan doors either, so no positive defect can be confirmed.

window_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: indeterminate. No frames, glass, or voids visible; with no walls to host them windows cannot be assessed, and no orphan glass panels float, so no positive defect confirmed.

global_visual.evidence: Synthesis: two independent FAILs reinforce each other. The architectural shell (walls, doors, windows, continuous floor) is entirely missing AND the surviving furniture is a flat-shaded, untextured, shadowless placeholder. The render cannot be validated against a floor-plan PDF on either the geometric or the material/light dimension.

scale_rotation.evidence: Judge 1: relative proportions between bed, wardrobe, kitchen run, nightstands and dresser+mirror are internally consistent and share a coherent iso orientation; no mis-scaling or rotated-object error among the objects present.

wall_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1 (ESTRUTURA): zero vertical wall geometry anywhere — no perimeter, no partitions. The entire structural shell is absent, not a stub or a gap.

room_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no enclosed rooms. Furniture sits on two disjoint tan slabs with a gap, and the upper-left cluster (kitchen unit + colored blocks) has no slab beneath it. No room boundary or continuous floor plane is formed.

material_light.evidence: Judge 2 (MATERIAL & LUZ): flat-shaded SketchUp-style placeholder, not a V-Ray render. Solid per-face color fills with default directional face-shading, zero textures (no wood grain, no fabric), no specular/reflectance, and no cast/contact shadows or AO — furniture floats ungrounded. Exposure itself is fine, but material and light are placeholder-grade.

findings.location: entire perimeter — no walls rendered anywhere in the scene
findings.evidence: Zero wall geometry; perimeter fully non-enclosed and no partitions separate furniture clusters. The whole structural shell is missing, not just a segment. (Judge 1 vf_001) | FP-032: oracle FAIL degraded to WARN (backend not proven discriminative).

findings.location: upper-left cluster: kitchen cabinet run + purple/orange/blue blocks
findings.evidence: Cluster floats with no floor slab beneath it; only two separate tan slabs exist under the two bed groups. Floor is discontinuous/absent, so no coherent room floor plane is formed. (Judge 1 vf_002) | FP-032: oracle FAIL degraded to WARN (backend not proven discriminative).

findings.location: entire render — all furniture (kitchen unit, wardrobe, beds, nightstands, colored boxes)
findings.evidence: All geometry rendered as flat solid colors with default SketchUp face shading; no textures, no material maps, no specular/reflectance. Wood pieces are uniform brown/beige fills with no grain; bed surfaces flat with no fabric — the exact placeholder failure mode the material_light axis exists to catch. (Judge 2 vf_001) | FP-032: oracle FAIL degraded to WARN (backend not proven discriminative).

findings.location: ground/backdrop under every object
findings.evidence: No contact shadows, cast shadows, or ambient occlusion on the plain gray background; objects float with no light-derived grounding, consistent with a non-raytraced flat-shaded preview. (Judge 2 vf_002)

findings.location: low blocks left-of-center (purple, orange, blue boxes)
findings.evidence: Three boxes carry pure saturated placeholder hues (magenta-purple, orange, primary blue) reading as color-coded stand-ins, not realistic furniture materials; they clash with the warm brown/beige 'warm_compact' palette and reinforce the placeholder impression. (Judge 2 vf_003)

design_patterns_observed.pattern: furniture-only export with no architectural shell (walls/doors/windows/continuous floor absent)
design_patterns_observed.why: Combining Judge 1's total-shell failure with Judge 2's placeholder shading shows the pipeline emitted loose furniture with neither host walls nor a lit material pass. A floor-plan render must carry the enclosing shell as grounding context; furniture on disjoint slabs in a void cannot be validated against a PDF regardless of material quality.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: discontinuous floor slabs with floating furniture clusters
design_patterns_observed.why: Two disjoint tan slabs plus an un-slabbed cluster (Judge 1) means no single continuous floor plane. A room layout needs one coherent floor to establish adjacency and enclosure; fragmented slabs read as detached islands and defeat room detection.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: internally consistent iso orientation and furniture proportions
design_patterns_observed.why: Judge 1's scale_rotation PASS: bed, wardrobe, kitchen run, nightstands and dresser+mirror share one coherent iso projection with sane relative sizes. This is the one reusable positive — object-level scale/rotation modeling is sound even when the shell and materials are not, so the defect is isolated to shell + material passes, not object geometry.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: paleta warm_compact (brown/beige wood tones)
design_patterns_observed.why: Wardrobe, kitchen unit and bed frames share a coherent warm brown/beige family, but as flat fills with no texture the palette reads as intent rather than realized material — a viable palette awaiting a real material pass. (from Judge 2)

design_patterns_observed.pattern: default SketchUp directional face-shading with no ray-traced light
design_patterns_observed.why: Only per-face luminance steps (lighter tops, darker sides); no cast shadows, AO, or specular. Insufficient to distinguish a lit render from a flat preview — the signature of a skipped V-Ray/render pass. (from Judge 2)

design_patterns_observed.pattern: saturated primary-color placeholder blocks amid a neutral scene
design_patterns_observed.why: Pure purple/orange/blue boxes break material coherence and signal untextured color-coded stand-ins rather than finished furniture materials, clashing with the surrounding warm palette. (from Judge 2)

room_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no enclosed rooms — only two disconnected tan floor patches under two furniture clusters, no continuous floor plate, so room count/shape cannot be matched to the PDF.

wall_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1 (ESTRUTURA): zero wall geometry anywhere — no perimeter, no partitions, no vertical planes. Architectural shell entirely absent.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: paleta black_wood_gold
design_patterns_observed.why: Only 2 of the 3 named tones read in pixels — black chairs and brown wood are clear, but no gold/metallic surface appears; a 3-tone palette that relies on a metallic accent will read as incomplete when reflectance/specular is not rendered.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: luz — SketchUp default per-face shading (no GI, no cast shadows, no AO)
design_patterns_observed.why: Lighting limited to hardcoded lighter-top/darker-side face shading; without cast shadows or ambient occlusion nothing is grounded and depth reads flat — a placeholder-lighting signature, not a rendered light pass.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: material — solid untextured single-color fills
design_patterns_observed.why: Every material is a flat color with no texture map or reflectance, the defining trait of a flat-shaded placeholder rather than a rendered material.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: grounding — objects without an enclosing floor plate or contact shadow read as floating
design_patterns_observed.why: [synthesis: judge 1 fragmented/absent floor plate + judge 2 missing contact/AO shadow] Placing furniture over a void with only localized floor patches and no AO produces a floating, ungrounded layout; a continuous floor field plus contact shadow is what anchors clusters spatially.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: proportion — furniture-cluster internal scale
design_patterns_observed.why: [from judge 1] Beds, wardrobe, dining set and kitchen units hold believable relative proportions and consistent iso orientation even with the architectural shell absent; the furniture-massing proportion logic is sound independent of the envelope failure.

rooms: SALA DE JANTAR | SALA DE ESTAR

global_visual.evidence: Synthesis of both judges: the frame reads as an intermediate/geometry-stage artifact, not a finished render — flat SU shading with theme materials unapplied (judge 2) PLUS one furniture cluster with no floor slab under it (judge 1). Panel rule: FAIL because judge 2 FAILs; global coherence also fails as the scene does not read as a completed themed render.

door_fidelity.evidence: Per judge 1: no leaves/jambs/openings drawn because the shell is absent in this SU-free view. Door hosting is not assessable; no floating_door or misplacement observed. Not-evaluated-in-this-view, hence WARN.

wall_fidelity.evidence: Per judge 1 (ESTRUTURA): SU-free furniture-only iso — no wall shell rendered, so continuity/enclosure is not assessable from pixels. Absence is the render TYPE, not an erased segment; carried through as WARN (not a missing_wall FAIL).

room_fidelity.evidence: Per judge 1: two beige floor slabs host plausible bedrooms, but the upper/center-left cluster (kitchen + purple/orange/blue sofa modules + wardrobe) floats on bare gray void with no slab beneath — a floor_leak indicating a missing floor patch or furniture placed outside room bounds.

scale_rotation.evidence: Per judge 1: furniture proportions internally consistent, all items upright, uniform isometric projection across every object — no rotation, mirroring, or toppled geometry. Strongest axis of the render.

window_fidelity.evidence: Per judge 1: no walls to host glass, so no windows rendered and window fidelity is not assessable. No orphan_glass_panel or full_height_void present. WARN, not FAIL.

material_light.evidence: Per judge 2 (MATERIAL & LUZ, high confidence): every surface is flat/faceted default SU sun-shading on a #ccc void — zero texture/reflectance/specular, no cast or contact/AO shadows (furniture floats), and the named 'black_wood_gold' palette is absent (no black, no gold). Saturated purple/orange/blue blocks are unassigned SketchUp default component colors bleeding through. Reads as a flat-shaded placeholder, not a rendered material pass.

findings.location: upper / center-left furniture cluster (kitchen cabinets + purple/orange/blue sofa modules + wardrobe)
findings.evidence: Cluster sits on bare gray background with no beige floor slab, while the two bedroom clusters each rest on a slab — missing floor patch or furniture placed outside room bounds. (carried from judge 1)

findings.location: entire scene — all furniture volumes
findings.evidence: All surfaces are single solid colors with only default face-normal sun shading; no texture/bump/roughness maps — 'wood' wardrobes/kitchen read as flat plastic. Placeholder-level, not a rendered material pass. (carried from judge 2)

findings.location: ground plane under every object
findings.evidence: No cast shadows and no contact/AO shadows; objects float with no grounding — consistent with lighting solve disabled. (carried from judge 2)

findings.location: bottom-left colored blocks (purple/orange/blue) and overall palette
findings.evidence: Labeled 'black_wood_gold' but palette is brown/tan/beige with no black or gold; saturated purple/orange/blue are SketchUp default unassigned-material colors — theme material assignment did not reach those components. (carried from judge 2)

findings.location: whole frame
findings.evidence: Cross-report inference: judge 1 flags this as a SU-free geometry iso (walls absent by TYPE), yet judge 2 scored it against V-Ray render-fidelity criteria (textures/palette/shadows). If this render is BY DESIGN a geometry-only preview, material_light's FAIL is partly a category mismatch (flat shading expected) and would downgrade to WARN. The genuinely stage-independent defects remain: the floor_leak (vf_001) and default component colors bleeding through (vf_004) both point to an incomplete assembly pipeline — floor slabs and theme materials not yet applied to all components.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: black_wood_gold palette not applied — default component colors bleed through
design_patterns_observed.why: Named palette (black+wood+gold) is absent; flat brown/beige fills plus SketchUp default primary colors (purple/orange/blue) replace it. When material assignment misses components, raw default hues leak into the frame and masquerade as themed choices — a recurring failure signature to watch for.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: flat SketchUp sun-shading substituted for V-Ray render
design_patterns_observed.why: Only face-normal directional shading with no textures/reflectance/shadows — reads as placeholder. Useful as a diagnostic tell: if tops are uniformly lighter and sides darker with hard color boundaries and zero contact shadows, the render pass did not run.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: per-room floor slab generation incomplete under some clusters
design_patterns_observed.why: Bedrooms get slabs but the kitchen/living cluster floats on void (judge 1 floor_leak). Combined with judge 2's unassigned materials on the SAME cluster, indicates a build-order/coverage gap where later-placed room groups miss both slab and material passes — check that slab+material application iterates over ALL room groups, not a subset.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: uniform isometric projection preserves relative-scale legibility
design_patterns_observed.why: A single consistent iso projection kept beds, wardrobes, kitchen run and nightstands at correct relative proportions with all items upright (scale_rotation PASS). Reusable: iso geometry previews are reliable for scale/orientation QA even when materials/lighting are absent.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: missing contact/AO shadows make objects read as floating
design_patterns_observed.why: With no ground-contact or cast shadows, furniture loses visual grounding regardless of correct placement. Even a minimal contact-shadow / AO pass materially improves perceived groundedness — worth enabling before human visual review.

wall_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1 (ESTRUTURA): no wall geometry anywhere — zero vertical partitions, zero perimeter. Furniture floats with no partitions between kitchen/living/dining/bedroom groups. Architectural shell entirely absent.

door_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no doors present. With no host walls there is no surface for any opening; confirmed absence rather than a misplaced leaf.

global_visual.evidence: Both judges FAIL and agree independently: the entire architectural envelope (walls/floors/doors/windows/enclosure) is missing AND all surfaces are untextured flat fills with no lighting/shadow. This is a furniture-only, flat-shaded placeholder — not a valid floor-plan render.

window_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no windows present. The two upright reflective rectangles are standing mirrors on dresser bases (furniture), not architectural glazing; no wall exists to host a window.

scale_rotation.evidence: Judge 1: furniture-to-furniture proportions are internally plausible and orientation is a valid iso, but with no wall shell the placement/rotation cannot be confirmed against the plan; furniture reads as scattered rather than laid out inside rooms.

room_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no enclosed rooms — no continuous floor, no wall enclosure. Floor exists only as small disconnected rugs/patches, leaving most of the footprint void. Nothing reads as a room.

material_light.evidence: Judge 2 (MATERIAL & LUZ): every surface is a solid flat-color fill (beige/tan/mid-brown/dark-walnut) with zero texture, wood grain, reflectance or specular. Only SketchUp default directional face-shading is present; no cast shadows, contact shadows or ambient occlusion — a flat-shaded placeholder, not a V-Ray render.

findings.location: entire plan perimeter — no walls anywhere in the scene
findings.evidence: No wall segments rendered at all. Perimeter fully non-enclosed; furniture floats in open space with no partitions between kitchen, living, dining and bedroom groups.

findings.location: whole model — only isolated floor patches under a few furniture groups
findings.evidence: No continuous floor slab; floor exists only as small disconnected rugs/patches, leaving most of the footprint void. Rooms are neither floored nor enclosed.

findings.location: entire render
findings.evidence: Architectural shell entirely absent (walls, doors, windows, room enclosure). Furniture-only, so no geometric fidelity to a floor plan can be established — categorical structural failure.

findings.location: n/a — no doors present because there are no host walls
findings.evidence: No door geometry visible; with no walls any doorway would be orphaned. Confirmed absence rather than a misplaced leaf.

findings.location: all furniture (wardrobe, beds, kitchen cabinets, sofa, desk, dining table)
findings.evidence: All pieces render as untextured solid-color polygons; 'dark_walnut' appears only as a flat dark-brown fill with no grain, gloss or reflectance. No material/texture layer applied — a flat placeholder, not a photoreal render.

findings.location: entire scene / ground plane
findings.evidence: No cast shadows, contact shadows or ambient occlusion under any object. Every piece sits on a flat ~#d0d0d0 background with no grounding; lighting reads as ambient-only with no sun contribution.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: paleta dark_walnut (dark-brown + beige two-tone)
design_patterns_observed.why: Palette is internally cohesive — dark-brown walnut casework/bed frames with beige cushions and cabinet fronts reads as a plausible walnut scheme; hue is not wrong, but it is delivered as flat fill with no material depth.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: SketchUp default face-shading in lieu of V-Ray materials/luz
design_patterns_observed.why: Directional face-shading alone (no textures, no shadows, no reflectance) produces a wireframe-adjacent placeholder look — exactly the material_light failure mode this panel exists to catch.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: missing contact shadow / ambient occlusion → objects float
design_patterns_observed.why: With no contact shadow or AO under any object, every piece detaches from the ground plane; grounding shadow is the cheapest cue that separates a seated model from a floating placeholder, and its absence here reinforces the placeholder read.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: internally-consistent furniture scale without an architectural container
design_patterns_observed.why: Judge 1's scale_rotation WARN (furniture proportions cohere with each other) combined with the total absence of walls/floor shows that plausible furniture proportioning is worthless without a spatial shell to anchor it — the result is a decoration set, not a floor plan. Furnish the enclosure, do not render furniture in a void.

rooms: SALA DE JANTAR | SALA DE ESTAR

window_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no window voids or orphan glass panels; pale blue-gray verticals read as dresser mirrors, not glazing. No walls exist to host glazing — not positively confirmable, hence WARN.

global_visual.evidence: Combined: furniture scatter with no architectural shell (Judge 1) plus flat unlit placeholder materials and no shadows (Judge 2). Structurally not a habitable floor plan and not a rendered image — building envelope and light simulation both absent.

room_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no enclosed rooms; isolated rug/floor patches under some beds only, left kitchen+block cluster sits over bare background with no floor slab. Perimeter entirely non-enclosed; floor coverage discontinuous.

door_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no architectural doors present; only cabinet/wardrobe fronts (furniture). With no host walls, no hosted door is evaluable, but no floating doors seen either — not confirmable, hence WARN not PASS.

scale_rotation.evidence: Judge 1: furniture mutual proportions internally consistent (beds, wardrobe, kitchen run, nightstands) and isometric orientation coherent across all objects — no tilted/flipped/mis-scaled pieces.

wall_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: zero vertical wall planes rendered anywhere — no perimeter, no partitions; furniture floats on blank gray background. Entire wall geometry absent.

material_light.evidence: Judge 2: every surface is flat solid-color fill with only SketchUp default per-face normal shading — no texture maps, no reflectance/gloss, no V-Ray response. 'dark_walnut' is flat mid-brown with no grain; saturated purple/orange/blue placeholder hues on bench; no cast/contact shadows or AO — an OpenGL flat-shaded viewport dump, not a lit render.

findings.location: entire scene perimeter
findings.evidence: Zero wall segments rendered anywhere; the enclosing perimeter is absent, leaving furniture floating on blank background. (Judge 1)

findings.location: left furniture cluster (kitchen run + purple/orange/blue blocks)
findings.evidence: Cluster has no floor slab while a separate rug/floor patch exists only under central/right beds — floor coverage discontinuous and unbounded by any walls. (Judge 1)

findings.location: all furniture (wardrobe, kitchen unit, beds, dressers, couch)
findings.evidence: Solid untextured color fills with basic face-normal shading only; no diffuse texture, no wood grain on walnut, no fabric weave — flat-shaded placeholder, not a V-Ray render. (Judge 2)

findings.location: entire scene / ground plane
findings.evidence: No cast shadows, contact shadows, or ambient occlusion under any object; every piece floats against flat gray with no grounding — confirms no light simulation ran. (Judge 2)

findings.location: low couch/bench cluster (center-left)
findings.evidence: Bench segments are pure saturated purple/orange/blue default placeholder colors, inconsistent with 'dark_walnut' intent and with plausible upholstery. (Judge 2)

findings.location: whole render
findings.evidence: No architectural envelope present, so wall/door/window fidelity cannot be positively confirmed against the PDF — only furniture geometry is judgeable and reads internally consistent. Combined with Judge 2's no-shadow/no-texture finding, this is a viewport export rather than a render. (Judges 1+2)

design_patterns_observed.pattern: paleta: 'dark_walnut' variant rendered as flat mid-brown with no grain/reflectance
design_patterns_observed.why: Wood material intent conveyed only as a solid brown fill, so the walnut identity is not visually readable.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: luz: SketchUp default face-normal shading with no cast/contact shadows or AO
design_patterns_observed.why: Lighting limited to per-face brightness from mesh normals; absence of any shadow makes the scene read as a flat-shaded viewport dump, not a lit render.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: material: saturated primary (purple/orange/blue) placeholder colors on furniture
design_patterns_observed.why: Unmixed default hues are a signature of unassigned placeholder materials rather than intentional upholstery.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: pipeline/layout: furniture-only OpenGL viewport export presented as a V-Ray render — no architectural envelope and no light sim
design_patterns_observed.why: Judge 1 (missing walls/rooms/floor) and Judge 2 (no textures/shadows) together indicate the export path emitted a raw SketchUp furniture viewport, not a rendered habitable floor plan; a recurring anti-pattern where the render/export stage is skipped or misrouted.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: proportion: furniture mutual scale and isometric orientation internally consistent even in placeholder mode
design_patterns_observed.why: Scale/rotation passed independently of material and render failures, showing the geometry-proportion stage is sound and can be trusted separately from the material/lighting pipeline.

rooms: SALA DE JANTAR | SALA DE ESTAR

room_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no enclosed room volumes; furniture clusters into ~2 zones each on its own separate floor slab with a visible gap. Rooms are unbounded and the floor plate is discontinuous.

window_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no windows, glazing or full-height voids visible; small nightstand mirrors are furniture, not glazing. Nothing to confirm or refute — not evaluable.

door_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1: no doors and no host walls to place them on; no orphaned/floating door leaf present. No positive defect, but door placement is not evaluable in a shell-less render.

scale_rotation.evidence: Judge 1: furniture is at mutually consistent human scale (double beds, tall wardrobe, kitchen run with uppers, proportional nightstands); orientations plausible, no gross flip or blow-up.

wall_fidelity.evidence: Judge 1 (ESTRUTURA): zero vertical wall planes anywhere; perimeter fully open, furniture rests on disconnected floor slabs. Consistent with an SU-free furniture-only iso, but as pixels the wall shell is wholly absent.

global_visual.evidence: Synthesis: both judges independently read this as an SU-free, flat-shaded, furniture-only iso on a bare canvas. Individual models render cleanly and at coherent scale, but with no architectural shell and no materials/shadows, global geometric fidelity vs a walled PDF plan cannot be confirmed — not evaluable, hence WARN rather than a confirmed geometric pass.

material_light.evidence: Judge 2 (MATERIAL & LUZ, high confidence): flat-shaded SketchUp default preview, not a V-Ray render. Zero texture (no wood grain, fabric weave or floor pattern), zero reflectance/gloss, and no cast/contact shadows — the placeholder/flat-shaded signature this axis exists to catch. Also three saturated primary-ish placeholder blocks (purple/orange/blue) breaking the warm palette.

findings.location: entire perimeter — no wall planes rendered anywhere in the iso
findings.evidence: Zero walls present; perimeter fully non-enclosed, furniture resting on open floor slabs. Whole architectural shell absent (SU-free furniture-only render).

findings.location: entire scene — kitchen cabinets, beds, wardrobe, sofas/blocks
findings.evidence: All geometry rendered with flat solid colors and default SU face-shading only. No texture maps, no specular/reflectance response anywhere — indistinguishable from a flat-shaded placeholder rather than a lit V-Ray render.

findings.location: gap between the left furniture-group slab and the right bedroom slab
findings.evidence: Floor is split into two disconnected slabs with an unfilled gap rather than one continuous plate; rooms are neither enclosed nor joined.

findings.location: ground plane under all furniture groups
findings.evidence: No cast or contact shadows and no ambient occlusion at contact points; only face-orientation tinting is present, so furniture reads as floating on a bare uniform gray background.

findings.location: left cluster — purple, orange and saturated-blue blocks
findings.evidence: Three highly saturated primary-ish blocks break the otherwise warm wood/tan palette and read as default/placeholder material assignments rather than plausible upholstery hues.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: flat-shaded SU-free preview (no V-Ray materials)
design_patterns_observed.why: No textures, no reflectance, no cast shadows — only default face tinting; the render is indistinguishable from a placeholder on the material/light axis. Both judges independently arrived at this SU-free reading, making the signature robust.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: warm wood/tan palette on casework and wardrobe
design_patterns_observed.why: Cabinetry, wardrobe and bed frames share a coherent warm brown/cream family, but it is expressed as flat fill color, not material, so it carries no fidelity weight.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: saturated placeholder-color blocks amid neutral furniture
design_patterns_observed.why: Purple/orange/blue blocks clash with the warm neutrals and signal unassigned/default colors rather than intentional material choices — a reliable tell for un-materialed geometry.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: furniture-only iso with no architectural shell on a discontinuous floor plate
design_patterns_observed.why: Combining judge 1's wall+room FAILs with judge 2's missing-shadow finding: with no enclosing walls and two disconnected slabs, furniture floats and rooms are unbounded — geometry cannot be validated against a walled PDF plan and the scene reads as un-anchored.

design_patterns_observed.pattern: internally consistent human-scale furniture across kitchen and bedrooms
design_patterns_observed.why: Despite the shell and material failures, beds, wardrobe, kitchen run and nightstands hold a coherent mutual scale with plausible orientations — a positive proportion pattern worth preserving even when the render pipeline is otherwise broken.
